summary: Gruber notes a reader's observation that iTunes uses the same interface pattern he had previously discussed in Safari: displaying a grayed-out text label inside an edit field when a search filter is selected. This brief post serves as a correction or addendum to his earlier comparison of iTunes and Safari interfaces, acknowledging that the two apps share more UI patterns than he initially realized.
key_points: iTunes uses the same grayed-out text label inside search fields that Safari does when a filter is selected
This observation came from reader Erik Barzeski, showing the value of reader feedback in interface analysis
The post is a brief correction to Gruber's earlier piece comparing iTunes and Safari interface details
money_quotes: when you choose anything other than "All" in the pop-up menu in the Search field, iTunes does the same thing Safari does, putting a grayed-out text label inside the edit field
key_insight: Even a careful observer of Apple's UI details can miss shared interface patterns across the company's own apps, as iTunes and Safari used the same search field labeling convention.
